name: The Instrument Room, Sandusky Junction Depot
description: A narrow room off the main waiting hall, lit by two oil lamps and dominated by the telegraph table — the brass key and sounder sit on green baize, the sounder's tin resonator amplifying every click into the low ceiling.
atmosphericDetails: The sounder clicks in irregular bursts — the Huron station is asking for a repeat.
A smell of hot metal and lamp oil hangs in the air, mixed with the coal smoke drifting in from the platform.
The codebook lies open on the corner of the table, its spine cracked from heavy use, one page corner folded down.
